title: The Motley Fool Interviews Rocket Lab CEO Peter Beck
text: Rocket Lab appears capable of establishing a pathway to profitability over the next five years. Neutron is the cornerstone of the money-losing company's plans to leap to profitability, as it will enable Rocket Lab to enter the market for larger, and more lucrative, payload launches. Rocket Lab closed out fiscal year 2025 with a 38% revenue growth at $602 million and a valuation at $44.6 billion. However, Rocket Lab continues to burn through cash with an operating loss of $51 million in the fourth quarter alone. During the company's post-earnings conference call, CEO Peter Beck told analysts Neutron's first launch is now expected during the fourth quarter of 2026. Beck warned, there's no fat in the schedule, so everything has to go according to plan. Beck shares insights on how Rocket Lab's end-to-end space service model—covering everything from launches to satellite systems—supports the company's long-term goal of profitability and industry leadership. Being a public company forces Rocket Lab to have a very sharp eye to profitability, a very sharp eye to sustainability.
